title: Multi-Class Text Labeling
concept: Multi-class classification assigns a document to one of many categories (e.g. sports, politics, tech) using softmax probabilities.

exercises.prompt: How many categories (classes) are involved in a multi-class text labeling task?
exercises.explain: Multi-class classification is used when we want to categorize text into one of three or more distinct classes, such as different news genres.

cards.title: Where Does This Article Go?
cards.body: When you browse a news app, articles are automatically sorted into sections like Tech, Sports, or World News. How does an AI read a random sentence and immediately know which drawer it belongs to? Let's find out!

cards.title: What is Multi-Class Labeling?
cards.body: Multi-class text classification is a technique where an AI reads a piece of text and assigns it to exactly one category from a list of three or more choices. It uses a mathematical tool called 'softmax' to give a percentage score to each category, ensuring they all add up to 100%.

cards.title: The Sorting Cabinet
cards.body: Imagine sorting mail into a sorting bin. Instead of just deciding 'junk' or 'not junk' (binary classification), you have a huge cabinet with labels like 'Bills', 'Letters', and 'Magazines'. You look at each piece of mail and drop it into exactly one of these many labeled drawers.

cards.title: Sorting a News Headline
cards.body:
An AI reads the sentence: 'The team scored a touchdown in the final seconds of the game.' 

The model outputs these confidence scores:
• Sports: 92%
• Politics: 2%
• Technology: 6%

Because 'Sports' has the highest score, the AI labels the text as Sports!

cards.title: One Label Only!
cards.body: People often think that multi-class classification means an article can belong to multiple categories at the same time. In standard multi-class classification, the AI must choose exactly one final category, even if a story about a new sports app fits both 'Tech' and 'Sports'.

cards.title: Two vs. Many
cards.body: See the difference between deciding between two options versus choosing from many.

left.text: Only two choices (e.g., Spam vs. Not Spam). Like a simple yes/no switch.

right.text: Three or more choices (e.g., Tech vs. Sports vs. Finance). Uses softmax to pick the single best fit.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following tasks is an example of multi-class text classification?

options.text: Sorting emails into exactly two folders: Spam or Inbox
Sorting books into Science, History, Fiction, or Art
Tagging a photo with five different friends' names
Deciding if a movie review is positive or negative

exercises.explain: In multi-class classification, the model must choose exactly one category out of three or more possible options.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Multi-class classification is only used when you have exactly two possible categories to choose from.
exercises.explain: Binary classification handles exactly two classes (like yes/no). Multi-class classification handles three or more classes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of the probability calculation.
exercises.sentence: The AI uses the softmax function to calculate a percentage score for each category.
exercises.explain: The softmax function calculates the probability for each category, ensuring the scores for all options add up to 1.0 (or 100%).

exercises.prompt: An AI model is analyzing an article about e-sports. Which of the following outputs represents a valid multi-class probability prediction?
exercises.explain: Option B is generated by an AI because standard multi-class classification outputs a probability distribution where all percentages add up to exactly 100% (1.0). Option A adds up to 140% which is impossible for softmax, and Option C contains negative numbers which probabilities cannot have.

exercises.prompt: Your AI model is reading news articles and sorting them into 'Sports', 'Business', or 'Science'. When it reads an article, how does it choose the final category?

options.text: It assigns the text to all categories that score above 50%
It selects the single category that gets the highest probability score
It randomly picks a label if there are more than two categories
It groups the text into a new, unnamed category automatically

exercises.explain: A multi-class classifier calculates probability scores for every possible category, but ultimately assigns the single category that receives the highest probability score (the softmax output).
